functions.name: netdoc.utils.append_nornir_netmiko_tasks

functions.name: netdoc.utils.count_interface_neighbors
documentation: Given a Netmiko parsed output, count CDP/LLDP neighbors per interface.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.delete_empty_keys
documentation: Delete None values recursively from a dictionary.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.filter_keys
documentation: Return a dict with only allowed keys.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.get_diagram_interfaces
documentation: Get relevant Interface queryset according to Diagram settings.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.get_remote_lldp_interface_label
documentation:
Return the remote interface label.

None is returned if no valid interface is found.
The interface is selected based on port_id, port_description and system_description.
The function tries to detect if port_id or port_description contain the interface description.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.find_model
documentation:
Get most similar DeviceType (model) using Netbox devicetype-library.

See: https://github.com/netbox-community/devicetype-library

functions.name: netdoc.utils.find_vendor
documentation:
Get most similar Manufacturer (vendor) using Netbox devicetype-library.

See: https://github.com/netbox-community/devicetype-library

functions.name: netdoc.utils.incomplete_mac
documentation: Return True if the MAC address is incomplete (from ARP table).

functions.name: netdoc.utils.is_hostname
documentation: Return true if the hostname is valid.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.is_command_supported
documentation: Return true if the discoverer/command is supported.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.is_command_filtered_out
documentation: Test a command line against a filter and return True if the command must be skipped.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.log_ingest
documentation: Ingest a log calling the custom ingestor.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.normalize_hostname
documentation: Hostnames must be uppercase without domain.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.normalize_interface_label
documentation: Given an interface name, return the shortname (label).

functions.name: netdoc.utils.normalize_interface_mtu
documentation:
Normalize MTU value returning an Interger.

None is returned if mtu is empty or None.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.normalize_interface_status
documentation:
Normalize interface status.

Return True if the link is up, False elsewhere.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.normalize_mac_address
documentation: Return a MAC Address in the format 01:23:45:67:89:AB.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.normalize_ip_address_or_none
documentation:
Return IP address (10.1.2.3) or None from string.

String can be 10.1.2.3 or 10.1.2.3/24.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.normalize_vm_status
documentation: Status must be offline, active, planned, staged, failed, decomissioning.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.normalize_vlan_list
documentation: Normalize a VLAN list (can be a comma separated string or a list).

functions.name: netdoc.utils.normalize_vlan_range
documentation: Normalize a single VLAN or VLAN range, returning a list of integer.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.object_create
documentation: Create a generic Model object using kwargs.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.object_get_or_none
documentation: Get a generic Model object using kwargs. None is returned if not found.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.object_list
documentation: Get a list of Model objects.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.object_update
documentation:
Update a generic Model object using kwargs. Object is saved only if modified.

With force=False, the field is updated only if not set.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.parent_interface
documentation: If subinterface return parent interface else return None.

functions.name: netdoc.utils.parse_netmiko_output

functions.name: netdoc.schemas.device.create

Create a Device.

A Device is created from hostname only, thus generic
model/tyoe/manufacturer/role are used. They can be updated later.
Before need to get or create Manufacturer, DeviceModel, DeviceRole and DeviceType.

Create a VirtualMachine.

A VirtualMachine is created from hostname, status, and device_id.
VirtualMachine is associated to a device, the device is associated

modules.name: netdoc.tests.test
documentation:
Clean previous data, load lab-specific content, and test.

To make the process faster, the database is created once and resued:
    - /opt/netbox/venv/bin/python3 /opt/netbox/netbox/manage.py test netdoc --verbosity=2 --keepdb

modules.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_reports
documentation: Script used to check NetDoc integrity.

classes.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_reports.InterfaceLabels
documentation: Check current Interface label compared to Interface name.

classes.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_reports.VRFIpPrefixIntegrityCheck

methods.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_reports.VRFIpPrefixIntegrityCheck.test_ip_address_uniqueness
documentation: Test IPAddress uniqueness within same VRF and Interface.

methods.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_reports.VRFIpPrefixIntegrityCheck.test_prefix_uniqueness
documentation: Test Prefix uniqueness within same VRF.

methods.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_reports.VRFIpPrefixIntegrityCheck.test_vrf
documentation: Test VRF between Interface and Interface's IPAddress.

methods.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_reports.VRFIpPrefixIntegrityCheck.test_interface_prefix
documentation: Test Interface's IPAddress owned by a Prefix.

methods.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_reports.VRFIpPrefixIntegrityCheck.test_address_prefix
documentation: Test IPAddress owned by a Prefix.

Check integrity between VRF, Interface, IPAddress, and Prefix.

The report verifies that:
* Interface.VRF == Interface.IPAddress.VRF -> warning if not
* Interface.IPAddress.VRF in Prefix.VRF -> error if not
* IPAddress uniqueness (within same VRF, Interface)

If Interface.VRF != Interface.IPAddress.VRF the user should check if this is expected
(e.g. when Interface is configured in global but the IPAddress is part of a VRF).

If Prefix.VRF does not exist for Interface.IPAddress.VRF the user should create it.

classes.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_reports.IPAMFromARP

methods.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_reports.IPAMFromARP.test_ipam
documentation:
For each ARP, check if an IPAddress exist.

ARPTableEntry.Interface.IPAddress.VRF must be equal to IPAddress.VRF.
Check corrispondence between ARP table and IPAM.

Dictionaries.

modules.name: netdoc.models
documentation:
Models (ORM).

Define ORM models for NetDoc objects:
* A Discoverable is a device willed to be discovered using a specific mode.
* Credential is associated to one or more Discoverables.
* A DiscoveryLog is the output for a specific discovery command executed in a Discoverable.

classes.name: netdoc.models.ArpTableEntry

methods.name: netdoc.models.ArpTableEntry.meta_interface
documentation:
Define meta_interface property.

meta_interface return Device or VM, if set.

methods.name: netdoc.models.ArpTableEntry.meta_role
documentation:
Role meta_role property.

meta_role return device/vm role, if set.

methods.name: netdoc.models.ArpTableEntry.meta_device
documentation:
Define meta_device property.

meta_device return Device or VM, if set.

methods.name: netdoc.models.ArpTableEntry.__str__
documentation: Return a human readable name when the object is printed.

Model for ArpTableEntry.

Each ARP seen on each network interface is
counted. One IP Address can be associated to one MAC Address. One MAC
Address can be associated to multiple IP Addresses.

documentation: Return a human readable name when the object is printed.

documentation: : False if output is unsupported (won't be ingested)

class_variables.name: netdoc.models.DiscoveryLog.parsed
documentation: : True if parsing raw_output return a valid JSON

class_variables.name: netdoc.models.DiscoveryLog.ingested
documentation: : True if all data are ingested without errors

classes.name: netdoc.models.MacAddressTableEntry

methods.name: netdoc.models.MacAddressTableEntry.__str__
documentation: Return a human readable name when the object is printed.

Model for MacAddressTableEntry.

Each MAC Address seen on each network
interface is counted. One IP Address can be associated to one AC
Address. One MAC Address can be associated to multiple IP Addresses.

Each route has a destination, type (connected, static...),

methods.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_scripts.IPAMFromARP.run
documentation:
For each ARP, check if an IPAddress exist and create it if it doesn't.

ARPTableEntry.Interface.IPAddress.VRF must be equal to Prefix.VRF.
Create IP Address on IPAM based on ARP table.

classes.name: netdoc.jobs.netdoc_scripts.FixData
documentation: Fix Device names and Interface labels.

functions.name: netdoc.ingestors.netmiko_cisco_nxos_show_vlan.ingest

VLAN - Interface association is ingested in the "show interface switchport" output.

modules.name: setup
documentation:
NetDoc PYPI setup file.

Install with: python3 setup.py install
Develop with: python3 setup.py develop
Make it available on PIP with:
    python3 setup.py sdist
    pip3 install twine
    twine upload dist/*
